raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, while I was in XXXX XXXX on an extended stay from XXXX to XXXX, an unauthorized fraudulent charge of approximately {$77.00} was made against a Wayfair XXXX for which I had applied in XXXX. I had applied for the card in order to get a discount while shopping on the Wayfair website, but never received the physical card and never made a charge against the account. 

Wayfair Citi XXXX contacted me in XX/XX/XXXX about a past due balance for a {$77.00} charge made in a sporting goods store somewhere in the United States. I told the agent that the {$77.00} charge was fraudulent. Because the card was compromised and the fact I had not received the card, I closed the account at that time. An investigation was conducted and a credit for the full amount was subsequently issued. I considered the matter closed. 

The charge reappeared a couple months later and I am now being billed for the original amount of the fraudulent charge plus exorbitant penalties and interest. I have spoken numerous times with Wayfair Citi XXXX billing agents and XXXX times with agents in the fraud investigation group to get the charge removed. Despite their promises to remove the charge and to correct the erroneous past due amounts on my credit reports, there have been no results and my XXXX XXXX has tanked from XXXX to the low-mid XXXX. I have written to several Citi agencies as well and gotten no response. 

I subsequently filed disputes with all XXXX major credit reporting agencies, but they responded that Wayfair Citi XXXX told them the charges are accurate. I am now at a loss on how to get my credit report and XXXX XXXX corrected.
